questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: It was ______ (embarrass) for Reeta to hear herself praised in public.
questions.explanation: We use the present participle 'embarrassing' because it describes the EFFECT of the experience on Reeta. The situation caused embarrassment, so it was embarrassing.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: The children were ______ (amuse) by the puppet show they saw yesterday.
questions.explanation: We use the past participle 'amused' because the children experienced the feeling. They were the ones who felt amusement, so they were amused.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: Ashna told me a ______ (move) story about her childhood.
questions.explanation: We use the present participle 'moving' because the story had the effect of moving (emotionally affecting) the listener. The story causes the emotion, so it is moving.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: Abdul is ______ (courageous) than Karim.
questions.explanation: Adjectives with three or more syllables form the comparative by adding 'more' before them. 'Courageous' has three syllables, so the comparative is 'more courageous'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: How is your brother today? Is he ______ ? (good)
questions.explanation: 'Good' has an irregular comparative form: good → better → best. Since we are comparing his condition today with before, we use the comparative 'better'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: Hari is the ______ (idle) boy in the class.
questions.explanation: 'The ... in the class' indicates the superlative degree. 'Idle' ends in -e, so we add -st to form 'idlest'. (Some accept 'most idle' as well.)

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: Your knife is sharp, but mine is ______ (sharp).
questions.explanation: Two things are being compared (your knife and mine), so we use the comparative degree. 'Sharp' is a short adjective, so we add '-er' to form 'sharper'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: Iron is a very ______ (useful) metal indeed.
questions.explanation: No comparison is being made here. The word 'very' modifies the positive degree of the adjective. So we simply use the adjective 'useful' in its positive form.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: What a ______ (fascinate) story!
questions.explanation: We use the present participle 'fascinating' because the story causes fascination. The -ing form describes the thing that produces the feeling.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct form: All the people looked ______ (worry) as they sat waiting for news.
questions.explanation: We use the past participle 'worried' because the people experienced the feeling of worry. The -ed form describes the person who feels the emotion.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct degree: The day 15th August 1947 will be remembered as the ______ (memorable) day in the history of India.
questions.explanation: 'The ... in the history of India' indicates the superlative degree. 'Memorable' has four syllables, so the superlative is formed by adding 'most' before it: 'most memorable'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct degree: There was nothing ______ (good) than the pizza I ate.
questions.explanation: 'Good' has an irregular comparative form: good → better → best. The word 'than' signals a comparison, so we use the comparative 'better'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct degree: There were ______ (few) people at the venue than expected.
questions.explanation: 'Few' has an irregular comparative form: few → fewer → fewest. The word 'than' signals a comparison, so we use the comparative 'fewer'. Note: 'fewer' is used for countable nouns (people), while 'less' is used for uncountable nouns.
